summary:
The primary focus is identifying an effective non-crossing property; thus, Carbachol was ruled out because it does not meet this criterion despite initial confusion from some sources suggesting otherwise based primarily on limited studies rather than comprehensive data sets indicating consistent behavior across various conditions including pregnancy where avoidance would be paramount. Ultimately, glycopyrronium remains unique by successfully meeting all outlined requirements making it ideal under most circumstances when considering safety profiles related directly back to preventing adverse interactions within critical physiological systems protected via natural selective permeability constraints imposed biologically between maternal circulation versus fetal/neural
